item.title: Stress Test - Illustrative vs Factual Examples in Legal Knowledge
item.description: Stress Test: Illustrative vs Factual Examples in Legal Knowledge Seed Rule Being Tested Rule: Distinguish illustrative examples from factual examples — mark hypothetical/teaching examples as such, date real-world examples.

item.title: README

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Visual Literacy and Aesthetic Evaluation Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Visual Literacy and Aesthetic Evaluation Knowledge The Gap The Seed covers: Subjective knowledge in creative domains (claim-type: subjective, consensus:) Sensory knowledge (taste, smell, texture) with reference standards Craft criticism (verifiable technique) vs aesthetic judg...

item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Experiential vs Documented Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Experiential vs Documented Knowledge The Gap When stress-testing the Seed against music composition, a significant gap emerges: the Seed has no rule for handling knowledge that is fundamentally experiential vs.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Context Frames
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Context Frames When does the same core knowledge need different presentations for different contexts? How do you handle purpose-specific variants without fragmenting the vault? The Problem The Seed addresses expertise levels (beginner/intermediate/advanced) and user ...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Probabilistic Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Probabilistic Knowledge How do we handle knowledge that is true “most of the time” but has exceptions? The Problem The Seed has confidence markers (high/emerging/disputed/obsolete), but these don’t capture inherent probabilistic knowledge - knowledge that is legitimately uncert...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Tacit Knowledge Capture
item.description: created: 2026-03-10 Frontier Exploration: Tacit Knowledge Capture The Problem Some knowledge cannot be easily transferred through written language.

item.title: Stress Test - Confidence Markers Rule Across Domains
item.description: Stress Test: Confidence Markers Rule Across Domains Testing Seed rule: “Use confidence markers (high/emerging/disputed/obsolete) to signal reliability of knowledge.” This note consolidates stress tests from multiple domains (Legal, Scientific Research) to provide a comprehensive view of the Confiden...

item.title: Stress Test - Prerequisites Rule in Mathematics
item.description: Stress Test: Prerequisites Rule in Mathematics Domain Seed Rule Being Tested Rule: For notes tagged difficulty: advanced or covering multi-step concepts, include explicit prerequisites in frontmatter listing required background notes.
